mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces borrowing costs and boosts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 4.0% over 3 months signals deteriorating labor market conditions, prompting risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL > 255) combined with low policy rates (FEDFUNDS < 2.0%) creates a 'stagflation' risk environment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where both CPIAUCSL > 255 and FEDFUNDS < 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%) during high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 250) lead to higher discount rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where both CPIAUCSL > 250 and FEDFUNDS > 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO > 105) indicates stronger manufacturing activity, boosting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO > 105.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1 > 2.5% annualized) signals robust economic demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of instances where GDPC1 > 2.5% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS > 150k) indicates a tightening labor market, increasing corporate hiring and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of instances where PAYEMS > 150k.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in residential construction (HOUST > 1300k) and consumer spending (RSAFS > 500k) signals strong demand conditions, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where both HOUST > 1300k and RSAFS > 500k.
